Main finding: LLM-integrated applications blur data and instructions and can be remotely influenced through retrieved content
Limitation: Primarily attack demonstration rather than containment-attestation system
What it means for this paper: Positions VCA around downstream consequences after untrusted content enters the workflow

Method/Data: AgentDojo dynamic benchmark with realistic tasks and security cases
Main finding: Tool-using agents are vulnerable to prompt injection over untrusted data and need evaluated defenses
Limitation: Benchmark measures attack/defense behavior rather than signed evidence provenance
What it means for this paper: Supports external benchmark context and motivates consequence-level metrics

Main finding: Prompt injection and excessive agency are first-order LLM application risks
Limitation: Guidance document rather than empirical evaluation
What it means for this paper: Provides practitioner taxonomy for input/tool/output boundary discussion

Main finding: Canonical JSON supports repeatable hashing and signing over structured data
Limitation: Standard does not define the security policy around signed claims
What it means for this paper: Supports the VCA design choice of canonical metadata-only bundles

Main finding: Ed25519 provides public verification of signed evidence
Limitation: Primitive only; key management and claim semantics remain system responsibilities
What it means for this paper: Supports the external-verifier layer and explains what signatures prove and do not prove
